unlimited-concern-groups.signature: Unit test dispatching with no artificial group limit, batched approval gate
unlimited-concern-groups.description: Concern groups are dispatched without the 5-group cap, using batched execution with approval gate

framework-agnostic-runner.signature: Auto-detected test runner from project config (node --test, cargo test, pytest, go test, etc.)
framework-agnostic-runner.description: Test runner is auto-detected from project codebase or configured via testFramework field

test-framework-config.description: New config fields for specifying test framework and runner command

backward-compatible-node-test.description: Node.js projects with node:test continue to work identically -- auto-detection defaults to node --test

token-budget-awareness.description: Large concern group counts use batched execution to avoid context window exhaustion

detection-in-init.description: Test framework detection runs during /rapid:init research phase and stores result in config

definition.scope: Removes the 5-concern-group limit from the unit-test skill and makes the test runner framework-agnostic by auto-detecting the project's native test tooling during init research.

tasks.description: Remove 5-group limit and implement batched dispatch with approval gate
tasks.acceptance: Sets with 10+ concern groups dispatch correctly with user approval between batches

tasks.description: Make test runner auto-detected from project config files
tasks.acceptance: Python projects use pytest, Rust uses cargo test, Go uses go test, Node uses node --test

tasks.description: Add testFramework detection to init research pipeline
tasks.acceptance: After init, config.json contains testFramework and testRunner fields

tasks.description: Update unit-test skill to use detected/configured runner
tasks.acceptance: Generated test commands use the configured runner, not hardcoded node --test

acceptance: No artificial group limit -- dispatches as many groups as scoper produces
Test runner correctly auto-detected for Node.js, Python, Rust, Go projects
testFramework config field populated during init
Backward compatible: existing Node.js projects work identically
